summary: This cluster focuses on the biodiversity and distribution of freshwater microalgae, specifically the Euglenaceae family, in the Indian subcontinent. The research documented 60 taxa of euglenophytes, including the genera *Colacium*, *Lepocinclis*, *Phacus*, and *Trachelomonas*, collected from 30 distinct sites across a 155,842 km² area of Orissa State, India. The study employed precise GPS-based sampling to survey diverse habitats, primarily stagnant water bodies and paddy fields, highlighting the ecological preference of these organisms for nutrient-rich, lentic environments.
